prs.title: feat: implement smart prefetching for job listings

labels.name: size:L
labels.description: This PR changes 100-499 lines, ignoring generated files

prs.body:
## **User description** ## Description This PR implements intelligent prefetching for the Job Search page to significantly improve perceived performance and eliminate loading states during active search exploration. **Key Changes:** - **`usePrefetch` Hook:** Created a robust custom hook with a module-level `Map` cache that persists API responses. Includes in-flight deduplication and a 5-minute TTL cache expiry. - **JobSearch Integration:** Added `onMouseEnter` and `onFocus` event listeners to the Trending Search buttons to instantly trigger background data fetching on hover. - **Instant Rendering:** `JobSearch.jsx` now intercepts searches, checking the synchronous cache before setting a loading state, ensuring instant rendering on cache hits. ## Type of Change - [ ] Bug fix - [x] New feature - [ ] Documentation update - [x] Performance improvement - [ ] Other (describe) ## Related Issue Fixes #2779 ## Testing - [x] Unit tests pass - [x] Tested Locally - [x] New tests added **Test Output:** ```text > npx vitest run src/hooks/__tests__/usePrefetch.test.jsx ✓ src/hooks/__tests__/usePrefetch.test.jsx (5 tests) 27ms ✓ should fetch data when prefetchJobSearch is called ✓ should not fetch duplicate requests simultaneously (deduplication) ✓ should return cached data if available and fresh ✓ should clear cache on error ✓ should expire cache after TTL (5 minutes) Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 5 passed (5) ___ ## **CodeAnt-AI Description** Speed up job searches by loading trending queries in the background ### What Changed - Hovering or focusing a trending search now starts fetching results before the search is clicked - Repeating the same search can now show saved results immediately instead of showing a loading state again - Search results are stored briefly and expire after 5 minutes, so stale results are not reused - Failed prefetches are cleared so the search can try again later ### Impact `✅ Faster job search results` `✅ Fewer loading spinners during search` `✅ Quicker access to trending searches`
💡 Usage Guide ### Checking Your Pull Request Every time you make a pull request, our system automatically looks through it. We check for security issues, mistakes in how you're setting up your infrastructure, and common code problems. We do this to make sure your changes are solid and won't cause any trouble later. ### Talking to CodeAnt AI Got a question or need a hand with something in your pull request? You can easily get in touch with CodeAnt AI right here. Just type the following in a comment on your pull request, and replace "Your question here" with whatever you want to ask: 
@codeant-ai ask: Your question here
This lets you have a chat with CodeAnt AI about your pull request, making it easier to understand and improve your code. #### Example 
@codeant-ai ask: Can you suggest a safer alternative to storing this secret?
### Preserve Org Learnings with CodeAnt You can record team preferences so CodeAnt AI applies them in future reviews. Reply directly to the specific CodeAnt AI suggestion (in the same thread) and replace "Your feedback here" with your input: 
@codeant-ai: Your feedback here
This helps CodeAnt AI learn and adapt to your team's coding style and standards. #### Example 
@codeant-ai: Do not flag unused imports.
### Retrigger review Ask CodeAnt AI to review the PR again, by typing: 
@codeant-ai: review
### Check Your Repository Health To analyze the health of your code repository, visit our dashboard at [https://app.codeant.ai](https://app.codeant.ai). This tool helps you identify potential issues and areas for improvement in your codebase, ensuring your repository maintains high standards of code health.
## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Job search results are now cached with a 5-minute TTL to reduce redundant API requests * Popular search buttons now prefetch results on hover and focus for faster access * **Tests** * Added comprehensive test suite for the search caching functionality, covering successful fetches, request deduplication, cache retrieval, invalidation, and expiration

prs.title: fix(auth): implement single-device logout via jti blacklisting

labels.name: GSSoC'26
labels.description: Part of GirlScript Summer of Code 2026

prs.body:
Fixes #1725

## Description

This PR resolves the global session invalidation bug where logging out from a single device would forcefully invalidate all active sessions for the user across all devices.

The root cause was that `POST /api/auth/logout` unconditionally incremented the user's `tokenVersion`. This PR fixes it by:
- Adding a `BlacklistedToken` model to the database to track invalidated tokens by their JWT ID (`jti`).
- Automatically attaching a unique `jti` to all custom bearer tokens and NextAuth session cookies.
- Refactoring `POST /api/auth/logout` to only blacklist the current token's `jti` by default, leaving `tokenVersion` intact so other devices remain active.
- Maintaining the old `tokenVersion` increment behavior only for password changes and explicit "logout from all devices" requests.
- Adding a background service (`tokenCleanupService.ts`) to prune expired blacklisted tokens.

*(Note for reviewers: Requires `npx prisma migrate dev` before deploying to create the new `blacklisted_tokens` table).*

## Related Issue

Closes #1725 

## Type of Change

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] UI/UX improvement
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Tests

## Screenshots

N/A

## Testing

Describe the commands you ran and any manual verification performed.

- [x] Ran `npm run lint` *(Verified code complies with ESLint)*
- [x] Ran `npm run build` *(Verified TypeScript compilation via `tsc --noEmit` and successful Prisma client generation)*
- [x] Ran `npm run format`
- [x] Ran `git diff --check`
- [x] Verified the changed behavior manually, or wrote `N/A` for documentation-only changes *(Traced token lifecycle to confirm single-device logout only invalidates the targeted `jti`, while password changes still increment `tokenVersion` globally).*
- [x] Updated or added tests where appropriate, or wrote `N/A` with a reason *(Ran `npm run test` to verify existing tests pass without regressions).*

## Checklist

- [x] My changes are focused on the linked issue
- [x] I have reviewed my own code
- [x] I have not introduced unrelated formatting or generated-file changes
- [x] Documentation is updated if needed


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Logout per device or from all devices; single-device logouts now invalidate specific session tokens and yield appropriate success responses.
  * Session tokens include per-session identifiers to enforce device-specific invalidation.

* **Chores**
  * Automatic cleanup of expired blacklisted tokens.
  * Toast system refactored to a consistent UI primitive set for more reliable styling and behavior.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Fix page title issue by replacing react-helmet with Gatsby Head API
prs.body: ### Description Fixes the issue where contributor profile pages were not updating the browser tab title correctly. The problem was caused by react-helmet not working reliably with React 19, which prevented and meta tags from being injected properly. This PR replaces the Helmet usage with Gatsby’s Head API and moves all existing meta tags (title, og tags, etc.) into the Head export. ### Fixes Fixes #580 ### Screenshots (if applicable) <img width="1512" height="951" alt="Screenshot 2026-04-04 at 7 44 53 AM" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3e09ed32-dce5-49a0-b44e-452181cc6f75" /> ### Checklist - [x] PR title is clear and descriptive - [x] Changes follow project conventions - [x] No unrelated changes included - [x] Documentation updated if needed ### Additional Context <!-- Any extra information reviewers should know -->

prs.title: fix: update browserslist database to remove outdated caniuse-lite war…
prs.body:
fixes #502 

## Summary
This PR updates the Browserslist database to resolve the CI warning regarding outdated caniuse-lite data.

## Problem
GitHub Actions build logs showed:
warning Browserslist: browsers data (caniuse-lite) is 8 months old.

## Solution
Ran:
npx update-browserslist-db@latest

This updated caniuse-lite and refreshed browser compatibility data.

Refactored the sidebar navigation from a drawer layout to a dropdown component card design, improving visual consistency and user experience.

- **Converted drawer to dropdown**: Replaced drawer-style navigation with a collapsible card-based dropdown
- **Fixed component title display**: Sidebar header now correctly shows component titles from [antora.yml](cci:7://file:///Users/yatharth/Documents/GitHub/docs.jenkins.io/docs/images/antora.yml:0:0-0:0) (e.g., "Artwork", "User Documentation", "Tutorials")
- **Improved toggle behavior**: Arrow rotation now correctly indicates state (▶ collapsed, ▼ expanded)

prs.title: fix: remove target="_blank" and rel="noreferrer noopener" from all files

labels.name: documentation
labels.description: Jenkins documentation, including user and developer docs, solution pages, etc.

prs.body:
Fixes #8709

### Description:

This Pull Request removes all occurrences of target="_blank" and the associated rel="noreferrer noopener" (and variations) from the codebase. This ensures that all hyperlinks behave consistently, opening in the same tab/window unless the user explicitly chooses otherwise.

### Changes:

- HTML Templates: Removed attributes from content/_partials/_feedback-footer.html.
- Documentation: Updated content/doc/feedback-form.adoc and tutorials to remove raw HTML link attributes.
- Data Files: Sanitized HTML strings within weekly.yml and lts.yml changelogs.
- Scripts/Code Samples: Cleaned up JavaScript strings generating links in source-code-hosting documentation.


### Verification

- Automated Search: Performed a comprehensive grep search for target="_blank", rel="noreferrer noopener", and rel="noopener noreferrer". Confirmed zero remaining matches.

- Syntax Check: Manually verified that all modified files (AsciiDoc, HTML, YAML, JSX) retain valid syntax and structure.

- Local Build: Attempted make run but was limited by local environment constraints (Docker/Ruby version). Relied on strict static analysis and existing CI checks.

prs.title: Chore: Remove unused legacy i18n code (#757)
prs.body:
Fixes #757 


#### Describe the changes you have made in this PR - Cleanup: Removed Dead Code I removed several old i18n.js files and i18n folders that were no longer being used.

Why?
They were broken: these files tried to use a library called banana-i18n which is not installed in the project, so they would crash if run.
They were unused: the project now uses vue-i18n instead.
They were everywhere: I found and deleted these useless files in three places: the main app, v0, and v1.

What changed?
Deleted the following: src/simulator/src/i18n.js & i18n/v0/src/simulator/src/i18n.js & i18n/v1/src/simulator/src/i18n.js & i18n/
No feature code was touched. The app works exactly as before, just cleaner!


---

## Code Understanding and AI Usage

**Did you use AI assistance (ChatGPT, Claude, Copilot, etc.) to write any part of this code?**
- [x] No, I wrote all the code myself
- [ ] Yes, I used AI assistance (continue below)

**Explain your implementation approach:**
<!-- 
Describe in your own words:
- What problem does your code solve? it removes the unwanted code from the simulator codebase. Specifically, it removes legacy i18n.js files and directories in src/simulator/src, v0, and v1 which:
- What alternative approaches did you consider? Keeping the files: Leaving them as "legacy reference".
Why rejected: The code is broken (missing module imports) and untouched. Keeping dead code is bad practice and increases maintenance cognitive load.
- Why did you choose this specific implementation? For the sake of future safety and cleanliness of the codebase
- What are the key functions/components and what do they do? Removed: src/simulator/src/i18n.js and its counterparts in v0/v1.

This helps reviewers understand your thought process and ensures you understand the code.
-->

---

## Checklist before requesting a review
- [x] I have added proper PR title and linked to the issue
- [x] I have performed a self-review of my code
- [x] **I can explain the purpose of every function, class, and logic block I added**
- [x] I understand why my changes work and have tested them thoroughly
- [x] I have considered potential edge cases and how my code handles them
- [ ] If it is a core feature, I have added thorough tests
- [x] My code follows the project's style guidelines and conventions

---



Note: Please check **Allow edits from maintainers** if you would like us to assist in the PR.

* **Chores**

issues.title: UI Crash due to Missing Imports (FavoriteButton and Cpu icon)
issues.body:
Description: Two components attempt to render sub-components or icons that are never imported. This throws a React ReferenceError ("FavoriteButton is not defined" and "Cpu is not defined") and completely crashes the client-side render when users navigate to these pages.

Steps to Reproduce / Location:
`src/components/repository/RepositoryOverview.tsx,` line 402 (Missing FavoriteButton import).
`src/pages/Settings.tsx,` line 756 (Missing Cpu icon import from lucide-react).

Suggested Fix: Add the missing imports at the top of the respective files: import { FavoriteButton } from "@/components/..." and add Cpu to the lucide-react import destructing list.

issues.title: Global Session Invalidation on Logout
issues.body:
Description: The POST /api/auth/logout endpoint increments the user's tokenVersion in the database. Because tokenVersion is used as a global invalidation mechanism in the JWT validation callback, logging out from one device (e.g., a mobile phone) instantly invalidates all active sessions across all devices (e.g., desktop browser).

Steps to Reproduce / Location: `app/api/auth/logout/route.ts `(Line 18).

Suggested Fix: Implement a session blacklist (e.g., storing the JWT jti in a fast cache like Redis) or clear the session cookie locally via NextAuth without aggressively incrementing the global tokenVersion unless it's a "Logout everywhere" or "Change Password" action.

issues.title: Contributor profile pages show incorrect browser tab title (latest contributor instead of current profile)
issues.body: ### Reproduction steps - Open a contributor profile For example: (https://contributors.jenkins.io/pages/contributors/allan-burdajewicz/) - Observe the browser tab title - Compare it with the actual contributor name shown on the page ### Expected Results The browser tab should display the current contributor’s name Example: Allan Burdajewicz – Jenkins Contributor Spotlight ### Actual Results The browser tab shows the most recently added contributor’s name Example: `Jay Francis – Jenkins Contributor Spotlight` The page content itself is correct, only the tab title is wrong ### Anything else? _No response_

issues.title: Add Search Bar to Blog Authors Page
issues.body:
### Suggestion

This issue proposes adding a client-side search bar to the /blog/authors/ page to improve discoverability of authors.

As there are already a good number of blog contributors, which keeps growing, it becomes increasingly difficult to quickly locate a specific author. I would suggest a lightweight search feature would significantly improve usability without modifying existing data sources or backend logic.

### Links

https://www.jenkins.io/blog/authors/

### Are you interested in contributing to this improvement?

Yes, i had thought of an approach. Add a client-side search input above the author grid that:

- Filters author cards dynamically

- Matches against:
- - Author name

- Updates results in real time (no page reload)

issues.title: Add Threads (Meta) Social Icon Support for Contributor Cards
issues.body:
### What feature do you want to see added?

Contributor cards currently support a limited set of social media icons. Some contributors have provided their Threads (by Meta) profile links, but these are not rendered because there is no Threads icon supported in the codebase.

### Upstream changes

Add Threads (Meta) as a supported social platform by:

1. Introducing a Threads icon (SVG) consistent with existing social icons.

2. Mapping Threads URLs to this icon in the contributor card rendering logic.

3. Displaying the icon whenever a valid Threads link is provided.

issues.title: Replace outdated Twitter bird icon with X logo
issues.body:
### Reproduction steps

- Open the contributors spotlight page 

- Look for the Twitter social icon (author card and inside info).

- Observe that the icon is still the classic Twitter bird.

### Expected Results

- The UI should use the current X logo and naming for the Twitter/X platform.

- All visual references and accessible labels should reflect the new brand

### Actual Results

- The interface renders the legacy Twitter bird logo for the Twitter/X social link.

- Asset filenames, CSS classes, or icon references still use twitter/bird variants 

### Anything else?

The project still uses the old Twitter bird logo in the UI/assets, which is now outdated since Twitter was rebranded to X in 2023. This creates an inconsistent and outdated brand reference in the interface and documentation.

issues.title: Logo navigation broken on external subdomains (contributors, stories, plugins)
issues.body:
### Reproduction steps

1. Visit https://contributors.jenkins.io/ or https://stories.jenkins.io/ or https://plugins.jenkins.io/ |
2. Click the "Jenkins" logo (top-left)
3. **Expected**: Navigate to https://www.jenkins.io/
4. **Actual**: Remains on https://contributors.jenkins.io/

### Expected Results

The Jenkins logo should **always navigate to https://www.jenkins.io/** when clicked, 
regardless of:
- Current page domain
- Current page URL path
- Current subdomain

This is a standard web convention that all users expect.

### Actual Results

When users click the Jenkins logo on external subdomain pages:
- **Current Result**: The page remains unchanged; no navigation occurs
- **Problem**: Breaks the universal web convention where the logo acts as a "Home" button
- **User Impact**: Users cannot quickly navigate back to www.jenkins.io from these pages

### Anything else?

### Pages with Broken Logo Navigation (FAILING):
| Page | Domain | Current Behavior |
|------|--------|-----------------|
| Contributor Spotlight | https://contributors.jenkins.io/ | ❌ Stays on current page |
| Success Stories | https://stories.jenkins.io/ | ❌ Stays on current page |
| Plugins | https://plugins.jenkins.io/ | ❌ Stays on current page |

### Pages with Working Logo Navigation (PASSING):
| Page | Domain | Current Behavior |
|------|--------|-----------------|
| Blog | https://www.jenkins.io/blog/ | ✅ Navigates to homepage |
| Documentation | https://www.jenkins.io/doc/ | ✅ Navigates to homepage |
| Home | https://www.jenkins.io/ | ✅ Works correctly |

issues.title: Bug Report - Logo Navigation Inconsistency
issues.body: ### Problem with this page https://contributors.jenkins.io/ ### Expected behavior The Jenkins logo should **always navigate to https://www.jenkins.io/** when clicked. regardless of: - Current page domain - Current page URL path - Current subdomain This is a standard web convention that all users expect. ### Actual behavior When users click the Jenkins logo on external subdomain pages: - **Current Result**: The page remains unchanged; no navigation occurs - **Problem**: Breaks the universal web convention where the logo acts as a "Home" button - **User Impact**: Users cannot quickly navigate back to www.jenkins.io from these pages ### Pages with Broken Logo Navigation (FAILING): | Page | Domain | Current Behavior | |------|--------|-----------------| | Contributor Spotlight | https://contributors.jenkins.io/ | ❌ Stays on current page | | Success Stories | https://stories.jenkins.io/ | ❌ Stays on current page | | Plugins | https://plugins.jenkins.io/ | ❌ Stays on current page | ### Pages with Working Logo Navigation (PASSING): | Page | Domain | Current Behavior | |------|--------|-----------------| | Blog | https://www.jenkins.io/blog/ | ✅ Navigates to homepage | | Documentation | https://www.jenkins.io/doc/ | ✅ Navigates to homepage | | Home | https://www.jenkins.io/ | ✅ Works correctly | ### Possible solution Change the logo link from a relative URL to an absolute URL: ``` Jenkins ``` ### Are you interested in contributing a fix? Yes, I am interested in working

issues.title: Broken and outdated links in Documentation Structure section of README
issues.body:
### Reproduction steps

- Open the repository: https://github.com/jenkins-infra/docs.jenkins.io.


- Scroll to the Documentation Structure section in README.adoc (on the main repo page).


- Look at the bullet point describing Gatsby Documentation and the link shown there.


- Click the Gatsby README link that appears as:


`https://github.com/jenkins-infra/docs.jenkins.io/tree/main/site#readme/[https://github.com/jenkins-infra/docs.jenkins.io/tree/main/site#readme/](https://github.com/jenkins-infra/docs.jenkins.io/tree/main/site#readme/).`




- In the same section, click the Running the Project Documentation link that points to `https://github.com/jenkins-infra/docs.jenkins.io/blob/main/site/README.md.
`
​



### Expected Results

- The Gatsby README link is rendered with a duplicated URL and odd Markdown formatting:

```
https://github.com/jenkins-infra/docs.jenkins.io/tree/main/site#readme/[https://github.com/jenkins-infra/docs.jenkins.io/tree/main/site#readme/](https://github.com/jenkins-infra/docs.jenkins.io/tree/main/site#readme/)

```

This looks broken/confusing in the rendered README.  

- The Running the Project Documentation link leads to a 404 – page not found, because site/README.md no longer exists and there is no site/ directory in the repo (only docs/, playbook/, and ui/).

### Actual Results

### - The Gatsby documentation link should be a single, valid Markdown link, for example:
```
[Gatsby site README](https://github.com/jenkins-infra/docs.jenkins.io/tree/main/site#readme)
or another clean representation without duplication.

```

### - The Running the Project Documentation link should either:

- Point to an existing, up-to-date document that explains how to run the project (e.g., under docs/, playbook/, or ui/), or

- Be updated or removed if the old site/README.md is no longer relevant.


### Anything else?

- The README still references site/ paths even though Gatsby content appears to have been cleaned up from the repository.


- Fixing these links would help new contributors find the correct documentation for working with the site and running the project locally.

issues.title: Performance: Remove unused FontAwesome dependency from layout
issues.body:
### Suggestion

The `content/_layouts/frame.html.haml` file loads `font-awesome.min.css`, but a codebase scan reveals no usage of fa- classes in the content. Removing this unused asset will improve page load performance by eliminating an unnecessary network request and CSS parsing.

### Links

content/_layouts/frame.html.haml


### Are you interested in contributing to this improvement?

Yes, I have already prepared a fix.

issues.title: Add target="_blank" and rel="noopener noreferrer" to external links for security and UX
issues.body:
### Suggestion

External links on the Jenkins.io website don't consistently use `target="_blank"` and `rel="noopener noreferrer"` attributes. This creates security vulnerabilities and provides a poor user experience.
## ❌ Problem

1. **Security Risk**: Missing `rel="noopener noreferrer"` allows external pages to access the `window.opener` object, creating a potential security vulnerability
2. **Poor UX**: Users expect external links to open in new tabs, but currently they replace the current page
3. **Inconsistency**: Some external links have these attributes while others don't

### Links

## 📍 Affected External Links

- https://plugins.jenkins.io/ (Plugins site)
- https://github.com/jenkinsci (GitHub organization)
- https://community.jenkins.io/ (Community forum)
- https://stories.jenkins.io/ (Success stories)
- https://contributors.jenkins.io/ (Contributors)
- https://issues.jenkins.io/ (Issue tracker)
- https://ci.jenkins.io/ (Jenkins CI)
- https://stats.jenkins.io/ (Statistics)
- Social media: Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Mastodon, Bluesky, YouTube, Reddit
- Sponsor links: Atlassian, Datadog, DigitalOcean, Discourse, Fastly, IBM, Netlify, PagerDuty, Sentry, CD Foundation, etc.
- GitHub links: (ibain, diversion-scm-plugin, etc.)

### Are you interested in contributing to this improvement?

Yes, I am interested in solving this issue

issues.title: Chore: Remove unused legacy i18n code in simulator/src
issues.body:
The directory src/simulator/src/i18n and the file src/simulator/src/i18n.js appear to be dead code residues from a previous localization implementation.

Findings:

Missing Dependency: 
src/simulator/src/i18n.js imports banana-i18n, but this package is not present in package.json

- Zero Usage: Global search reveals that no other file in the src directory imports src/simulator/src/i18n.js

- Active System: The project currently uses vue-i18n configured in src/locales/i18n.ts

Proposed Action:
Delete src/simulator/src/i18n.js

issues.title: 🐞 Bug: Sidebar cannot be resized via drag handle

labels.name: pending triage
labels.description: issue yet to be reviewed by maintainers

issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [x] I have searched the existing issues.

### Describe the bug

The "Circuit Elements" sidebar on the left appears to have a completely fixed width. Attempting to drag the right edge of the sidebar does not change its width, making it difficult to adjust the workspace layout.

### Steps to Reproduce

Open the CircuitVerse Simulator.
Hover the mouse cursor over the vertical dividing line between the sidebar and the canvas.
Attempt to click and drag the edge to the right or left.

### Expected Behavior

Expected behavior: The sidebar width should increase or decrease corresponding to the drag action.

Actual behavior: The sidebar width remains static. The cursor may not change to a resize indicator, and dragging has no effect.

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Is the faced issue/bug related to the Vue simulator?

Yes

### Used Vue simulator with or without backend?

None

### Is the bug present only on the dev server, the build, or both?

None

### Device Information

```markdown
OS: macOS
Browser/Environment: Chrome / Web Simulator (running via npm run dev)
Version: cv-frontend-vue current main branch
Node: v24.10.0
```

### Additional Context

Tested in local Dev environment.

### Are you working on this issue?

Yes

issues.title: Implement pagination or virtualization for "Yearly" leaderboard
issues.body:
## Describe the bug

The "Yearly" leaderboard tries to render every single contributor for the past year at once. As the community grows, this list becomes very long, causing:

Slower initial page load and hydration.
Excessive DOM nodes leading to choppy scrolling.
High memory usage on the client.

## To Reproduce
Navigate to /leaderboard/year.
Inspect performance or scroll rapidly.
Actual Behaviour: All entries are rendered immediately.

## Expected behavior
mplement client-side pagination (e.g., 50 per page) or virtualization (windowing) to handle large datasets efficiently.

## Are you working on this issue? (Yes/No)
Yes
